task: COMPLETE ID-BASED ARCHITECTURE MIGRATION - ZERO TEXT-BASED REFERENCES
severity: CRITICAL - FULL ARCHITECTURAL TRANSFORMATION TO ID-BASED SYSTEM
context: NxtExpense currently relies heavily on text-based references (emails, role names, status text) throughout the database, backend, and frontend. This creates massive scalability issues: when employees leave, get promoted, or teams change, the entire system breaks. When workflow statuses need modification, code must be changed across DB, backend, and frontend. This migration eliminates ALL text-based logic and implements a pure ID-based relational architecture where EVERYTHING is referenced by IDs at the database level. Surface-level changes (renaming roles, restructuring workflows, changing approval chains) become trivial configuration updates instead of code rewrites.

mandate.iron_law_1: Database level: EVERYTHING references by ID - ZERO text matching, ZERO email matching, ZERO role name matching
mandate.iron_law_2: Workflow status: Completely redesigned to support L1/L2/L3/L4 approval hierarchy with ID-based status tracking
mandate.iron_law_3: Employee changes: Promotions, role changes, team moves must NEVER break existing data or logic
mandate.iron_law_4: Scalability: Adding new roles, approval levels, statuses, or workflow stages requires ONLY configuration data changes
mandate.iron_law_5: MCP usage: MANDATORY for every schema change, migration, and code refactor
mandate.iron_law_6: Follow copilot-instructions.md: All standards apply - file size limits, testing requirements, RLS policies, proper TypeScript
mandate.iron_law_7: Surface-level flexibility: Business teams must be able to rename roles, restructure approvals, modify statuses without touching code

text_based_foreign_keys.problem: approval_mail_id_level_1, approval_mail_id_level_2, approval_mail_id_level_3 are EMAIL STRINGS
why_catastrophic: When employee leaves company, all FK references break
When employee email changes, all approval chains break
Cannot enforce referential integrity with email strings
Cannot track approval history when approver changes
No way to handle interim approvers or delegation
Cannot query 'all claims I approved' efficiently
text_based_foreign_keys.correct_approach: employee_id INTEGER FK to employees table

text_based_role_logic.problem: Code checks designation === 'State Business Head' throughout codebase
why_catastrophic: Typos break logic ('State Business Head' vs 'State Buiness Head')
Case sensitivity issues
Cannot rename roles without find-replace across entire codebase
Cannot add role variations (Senior SBH, Junior SBH)
Logic scattered across 50+ files instead of one config table
text_based_role_logic.correct_approach: designation_id INTEGER FK to designations table, business rules in designation_config table

text_based_status_workflow.problem: Status values are text strings: 'Pending Approval', 'Approved', 'Rejected'
why_catastrophic: Cannot distinguish WHICH level approved/rejected (L1? L2? L3?)
Cannot track partial approvals (L1 approved but L2 pending)
Cannot implement multi-level approval workflows
Status changes require code changes in frontend and backend
Cannot query 'all claims pending at L2' efficiently
Audit trail is impossible - who approved at which level?
text_based_status_workflow.correct_approach: status_id INTEGER FK to claim_statuses table with hierarchical workflow support

why_catastrophic: Email changes break all permissions
Cannot delegate permissions temporarily
Cannot have multiple admins with same permission level
No role-based access control (RBAC)
Cannot audit who had what permissions when
email_based_permissions.correct_approach: role_id INTEGER FK to roles table, permissions table with role_permission junction

state_based_approval_routing.problem: state_mapping.json maps state NAME to approver EMAIL
why_catastrophic: State name changes break routing
Approver email changes break routing
Cannot handle multi-state approvers
Cannot track historical changes in approval hierarchy
No referential integrity
state_based_approval_routing.correct_approach: state_id INTEGER, approval_routing table with employee_id FKs

issues: Cannot track which level is currently reviewing
Cannot show 'L1 Approved, pending L2' status
Finance review is buried in generic statuses
No clear distinction between operational approvals (L1/L2) and financial approvals (L3/L4)

principles: Each approval level has explicit Approved/Rejected/Pending statuses
Status progression is hierarchical: Draft → L1 → L2 → L3 → L4 → Issued
Finance steps are clearly separated from operational approvals
Returned for Modification can happen at any level
All statuses are ID-based with metadata in claim_statuses table

submission_stage.description: Claim submitted by employee, awaiting L1 review

l1_approved.description: Approved by State Business Head, moving to L2

l2_approved.description: Approved by Program Manager, moving to Finance Review

l3_pending.description: Awaiting Finance team review and verification (L3)

l3_approved.description: Approved by Finance team, ready for payment processing

l3_rejected.description: Rejected by Finance team due to policy violation or documentation issues

l4_issued.description: Payment has been issued to employee

returned_for_modification.description: Claim returned to employee for corrections/additional information

status_transition_rules.principle: Status transitions are validated against claim_status_transitions table

claim_status_transitions.purpose: Define which status transitions are allowed and who can trigger them

sro_boa_abh.status_progression: SUBMITTED → L1_PENDING → L1_APPROVED → L2_PENDING → L2_APPROVED → L3_PENDING_FINANCE_REVIEW → L3_APPROVED_FINANCE → L4_PENDING_PAYMENT_PROCESSING → L4_ISSUED

sbh_zbh_pm.status_progression: SUBMITTED → L2_PENDING → L2_APPROVED → L3_PENDING_FINANCE_REVIEW → L3_APPROVED_FINANCE → L4_PENDING_PAYMENT_PROCESSING → L4_ISSUED

approval_level_mapping.implementation: designation_approval_flow table maps designation_id to required approval_levels

complete_id_based_schema_design.core_principle: Every table uses surrogate integer primary keys. Every relationship uses integer foreign keys. Zero text-based lookups.

employees.purpose: Central employee registry - SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for all employee data
columns: employee_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY -- Auto-incrementing ID
employee_code VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE NOT NULL -- External ID like 'NW0000023'
designation_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK designations(designation_id)
state_id INTEGER FK states(state_id) -- Can be NULL for all-state employees
status_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK employee_statuses(status_id) -- Active, Inactive, On Leave, etc.
critical_rules: Employee records are NEVER deleted - only marked inactive via status_id
Historical approvals MUST reference employee_id not email
When employee leaves, date_of_leaving is set, status_id changed to 'Inactive'
All historical claims still reference this employee_id

designations.purpose: Master table for all job titles/roles
columns: designation_code VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL -- 'SRO', 'BOA', 'ABH', 'SBH', etc.
designation_name VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL -- 'Student Relationship Officer'
critical_rules: Business logic NEVER checks designation_name text
All business rules reference designation_id
Renaming a designation only updates designation_name - NO code changes needed

columns: state_code VARCHAR(10) UNIQUE NOT NULL -- 'AP', 'TN', 'KA', etc.
state_name VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL -- 'Andhra Pradesh'

cities.purpose: Master table for cities (for outstation dropdowns)
columns: state_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK states(state_id)
UNIQUE(city_name, state_id) -- Same city name can exist in different states

roles.purpose: System roles for RBAC (separate from job designations)
columns: role_code VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL -- 'EMPLOYEE', 'APPROVER_L1', 'APPROVER_L2', 'FINANCE', etc.
critical_rules: Roles are system-level permissions, NOT job titles
One employee can have multiple roles via employee_roles junction table
Approval routing uses roles, not email addresses

employee_roles.purpose: Junction table for many-to-many employee-role relationship
columns: employee_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK employees(employee_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
role_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK roles(role_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
example_data: -- Ravinder Jangili is both Employee and L1 Approver
INSERT INTO employee_roles (employee_id, role_id) VALUES (1, 1), (1, 2);
-- Mansoor is Employee, L2 Approver, and Admin
INSERT INTO employee_roles (employee_id, role_id) VALUES (4, 1), (4, 3), (4, 6);

employee_statuses.purpose: Status values for employees (Active, Inactive, On Leave, etc.)
columns: is_active_status BOOLEAN DEFAULT false -- True if employee is working

approval_routing.purpose: Define who approves what based on designation and state - REPLACES EMAIL-BASED ROUTING
columns: submitter_designation_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK designations(designation_id)
submitter_state_id INTEGER FK states(state_id) -- NULL means applies to all states
approver_role_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK roles(role_id)
approver_designation_id INTEGER FK designations(designation_id) -- Optional filter
approver_state_id INTEGER FK states(state_id) -- Optional filter for state-based routing
example_data: -- SRO in Telangana → L1 approver must be SBH from Telangana
(1, 1, 2, 1, 2, 4, 2, true) -- submitter_designation=SRO, submitter_state=Telangana, level=1, approver_role=APPROVER_L1, approver_designation=SBH, approver_state=Telangana
-- SRO in any state → L2 approver is PM (Mansoor)
(2, 1, NULL, 2, 3, 6, NULL, true) -- submitter_designation=SRO, submitter_state=ANY, level=2, approver_role=APPROVER_L2, approver_designation=PM
-- SBH in any state → L2 approver is PM (Mansoor) - NO L1 needed
(3, 4, NULL, 2, 3, 6, NULL, true)
critical_rules: When claim submitted, query this table to determine required approvers by employee_id
NEVER hardcode approver emails
State-based routing uses state_id FK not state name text
Adding new approval rules requires ONLY new rows in this table

claim_statuses.purpose: Master table for all possible claim statuses - IMPLEMENTS NEW L1/L2/L3/L4 WORKFLOW
columns: status_code VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL -- 'L1_PENDING', 'L1_APPROVED', etc.
status_name VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL -- 'L1 - Pending Review'
approval_level INTEGER -- 1, 2, 3, 4, or NULL for non-approval statuses
is_approval BOOLEAN DEFAULT false -- True for L1_APPROVED, L2_APPROVED, etc.
is_rejection BOOLEAN DEFAULT false -- True for L1_REJECTED, L2_REJECTED, etc.
is_terminal BOOLEAN DEFAULT false -- True for final statuses (issued, rejected)
is_payment_issued BOOLEAN DEFAULT false -- True only for L4_ISSUED
requires_comment BOOLEAN DEFAULT false -- True for rejections and returns
display_order INTEGER -- For sorting in dropdowns/filters
seed_data: (1, 'DRAFT', 'Draft', 'Employee is creating/editing the claim', NULL, false, false, false, false, false, 'gray', 1, true)
(2, 'SUBMITTED', 'Submitted', 'Claim submitted, awaiting review', NULL, false, false, false, false, false, 'blue', 2, true)
(3, 'L1_PENDING', 'L1 - Pending Review', 'Awaiting State Business Head approval', 1, false, false, false, false, false, 'yellow', 3, true)
(5, 'L1_APPROVED', 'L1 - Approved', 'Approved by State Business Head', 1, true, false, false, false, false, 'green', 4, true)
(6, 'L1_REJECTED', 'L1 - Rejected', 'Rejected by State Business Head', 1, false, true, true, false, true, 'red', 5, true)
(7, 'L2_PENDING', 'L2 - Pending Review', 'Awaiting Program Manager approval', 2, false, false, false, false, false, 'yellow', 6, true)
(8, 'L2_APPROVED', 'L2 - Approved', 'Approved by Program Manager', 2, true, false, false, false, false, 'green', 7, true)
(9, 'L2_REJECTED', 'L2 - Rejected', 'Rejected by Program Manager', 2, false, true, true, false, true, 'red', 8, true)
(10, 'L3_PENDING_FINANCE_REVIEW', 'L3 - Finance Review', 'Awaiting Finance team review', 3, false, false, false, false, false, 'yellow', 9, true)
(11, 'L3_APPROVED_FINANCE', 'L3 - Finance Approved', 'Approved by Finance team', 3, true, false, false, false, false, 'green', 10, true)
(12, 'L3_REJECTED_FINANCE', 'L3 - Finance Rejected', 'Rejected by Finance team', 3, false, true, true, false, true, 'red', 11, true)
(13, 'L4_PENDING_PAYMENT_PROCESSING', 'L4 - Payment Processing', 'Finance processing payment', 4, false, false, false, false, false, 'yellow', 12, true)
(14, 'L4_ISSUED', 'L4 - Payment Issued', 'Payment issued to employee', 4, true, false, true, true, false, 'green', 13, true)
(15, 'L4_PAYMENT_FAILED', 'L4 - Payment Failed', 'Payment processing failed', 4, false, false, false, false, true, 'red', 14, true)
(21, 'RETURNED_FOR_MODIFICATION', 'Returned for Modification', 'Returned to employee for corrections', NULL, false, false, false, false, true, 'orange', 15, true)
critical_rules: Frontend NEVER hardcodes status dropdown - fetches from this table
Backend NEVER checks status === 'L1 Approved' - checks status_id === 5
Business can rename statuses by updating status_name - NO code changes
Adding new approval levels requires new rows - NO code changes

claim_status_transitions.purpose: Define allowed status transitions and who can trigger them
columns: from_status_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK claim_statuses(status_id)
to_status_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK claim_statuses(status_id)
requires_role_id INTEGER FK roles(role_id) -- NULL means employee can do it
is_auto_transition BOOLEAN DEFAULT false -- True for auto-progression (L1_APPROVED → L2_PENDING)
validation_rules JSONB -- Custom rules for this transition
example_data: -- Employee can submit draft
(1, 1, 2, 1, false, false, NULL, true) -- DRAFT → SUBMITTED (role: EMPLOYEE)
-- L1 Approver can approve or reject
(2, 3, 5, 2, false, false, NULL, true) -- L1_PENDING → L1_APPROVED (role: APPROVER_L1)
(3, 3, 6, 2, true, false, NULL, true) -- L1_PENDING → L1_REJECTED (role: APPROVER_L1, requires comment)
-- Auto-transition after L1 approval
(4, 5, 7, NULL, false, true, NULL, true) -- L1_APPROVED → L2_PENDING (auto)
-- Any approver can return for modification
(5, 3, 21, 2, true, false, NULL, true) -- L1_PENDING → RETURNED (role: APPROVER_L1, requires comment)
(6, 7, 21, 3, true, false, NULL, true) -- L2_PENDING → RETURNED (role: APPROVER_L2, requires comment)
critical_rules: Status transition validation uses this table - NO hardcoded if/else chains
UI enables/disables buttons based on this table
Adding new transitions requires ONLY new rows - NO code changes

claim_approvals.purpose: Track approval history for each claim - WHO approved WHAT and WHEN
columns: claim_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK claims(claim_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
approver_employee_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK employees(employee_id) -- ID not email!
approver_role_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK roles(role_id)
old_status_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK claim_statuses(status_id)
new_status_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK claim_statuses(status_id)
critical_rules: Uses approver_employee_id FK not email
Even if approver leaves company, historical records remain intact
Can query 'all claims approved by employee X' using employee_id
Audit trail is complete and traceable

designation_approval_flow.purpose: Map which designations require which approval levels
columns: designation_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK designations(designation_id)
required_approval_levels INTEGER[] NOT NULL -- e.g., [1, 2, 3, 4] or [2, 3, 4]
seed_data: -- SRO, BOA, ABH need all 4 levels
INSERT INTO designation_approval_flow (designation_id, required_approval_levels) VALUES (1, ARRAY[1,2,3,4]), (2, ARRAY[1,2,3,4]), (3, ARRAY[1,2,3,4]);
INSERT INTO designation_approval_flow (designation_id, required_approval_levels) VALUES (4, ARRAY[2,3,4]), (5, ARRAY[2,3,4]), (6, ARRAY[2,3,4]);
critical_rules: When claim submitted, determine status progression from this table
Changing approval requirements requires ONLY updating this table
Backend computes next required status dynamically

work_locations.purpose: Master table for work location types - REPLACES PostgreSQL ENUM
columns: location_code VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL -- 'OFFICE_WFH', 'FIELD_BASE', etc.
location_name VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL -- 'Office / WFH'
critical_rules: Claims table uses location_id FK not text enum
Form logic reads requires_* flags from this table
Adding new work locations requires ONLY new rows - NO code changes

vehicle_types.purpose: Master table for vehicle types - REPLACES PostgreSQL ENUM
columns: vehicle_name VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL -- 'Two Wheeler'

designation_vehicle_permissions.purpose: Define which designations can use which vehicles - REPLACES HARDCODED ARRAYS
columns: designation_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK designations(designation_id)
vehicle_type_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK vehicle_types(vehicle_type_id)
seed_data: -- SRO can only use two wheeler
critical_rules: Vehicle dropdown filtered by designation_id using this table
Backend validation checks this table - NO hardcoded designation checks
Changing vehicle permissions requires ONLY updating this table

expense_rates.purpose: Configurable expense rates - REPLACES MAGIC NUMBERS
columns: designation_id INTEGER FK designations(designation_id) -- NULL means applies to all
effective_to DATE -- NULL means currently active
critical_rules: ALL calculations read from this table - NO magic numbers like 120, 350, 1000
Rate changes tracked via effective_from/effective_to for historical accuracy
Can query historical rates for audit purposes

transport_types.purpose: Master table for transport types (Taxi, Bus, Train, etc.)

validation_rules.purpose: Dynamic validation rules - REPLACES HARDCODED CONSTANTS
columns: rule_code VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL -- 'MAX_CLAIM_DAYS', 'MIN_TAXI_AMOUNT', etc.
rule_value JSONB NOT NULL -- Flexible: can be number, string, object, array
seed_data: (1, 'MAX_CLAIM_DAYS', 'Maximum days in single claim submission', '{"value": 7}', 'Maximum date range for claim submission', true)
(2, 'FUTURE_DATES_ALLOWED', 'Allow future date claims', '{"value": false}', 'Whether employees can submit claims for future dates', true)
(3, 'FUEL_TAXI_MUTUAL_EXCLUSION', 'Fuel and taxi mutually exclusive', '{"value": true}', 'Cannot claim both fuel and taxi for same day', true)

(4, 'MAX_PRINCIPAL_FOOD_TIMES_PER_MONTH', 'Max food with principals per month', '{"value": 5, "applicable_designations": [3,4,5,6]}', 'Maximum times per month ABH/SBH/ZBH/PM can claim food with principals', true)
critical_rules: Backend validation reads rules from this table - NO hardcoded 7 days, etc.
Changing business rules requires ONLY updating rule_value - NO code deploy
JSONB allows flexible rule structures for complex validations

claims.purpose: Core claims table - FULLY ID-BASED, NO TEXT REFERENCES
columns: employee_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK employees(employee_id) -- ID not email!
designation_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK designations(designation_id) -- Snapshot of designation at claim time
claim_date DATE NOT NULL -- Single day this claim represents
work_location_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK work_locations(location_id) -- ID not enum!
vehicle_type_id INTEGER FK vehicle_types(vehicle_type_id) -- NULL if not applicable
outstation_city_id INTEGER FK cities(city_id) -- NULL if not outstation
from_city_id INTEGER FK cities(city_id) -- For intercity travel
to_city_id INTEGER FK cities(city_id) -- For intercity travel
status_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK claim_statuses(status_id) -- ID not text!
current_approval_level INTEGER -- 1, 2, 3, 4, or NULL if not in approval
indexes: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_claims_unique_employee_date ON claims(employee_id, claim_date) -- One claim per employee per day
critical_rules: ZERO text-based FKs - everything is integer IDs
Employee promotion doesn't break claims - designation_id is snapshot
Status changes use status_id not text matching
Outstation/city references use city_id not city names

claim_expenses.purpose: Detailed expense line items for each claim
columns: claim_id INTEGER NOT NULL FK claims(claim_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
transport_type_id INTEGER FK transport_types(transport_type_id) -- For taxi/transport

allowed_email_domains.purpose: Corporate email domain validation - REPLACES HARDCODED ARRAY

system_settings.purpose: Global system configuration - REPLACES HARDCODED CONSTANTS

phase_4_migrate_claims_table.priority: P1 - HIGH RISK - Affects existing data
critical_warnings: This migration modifies the core claims table
Must preserve all existing claim data

migrations.purpose: Safety: Create backup of existing claims table
content: CREATE TABLE claims_backup_pre_id_migration AS SELECT * FROM claims;

migrations.purpose: Add new ID-based columns to claims table
content: ALTER TABLE claims ADD COLUMN designation_id INTEGER;
ALTER TABLE claims ADD COLUMN work_location_id INTEGER;
ALTER TABLE claims ADD COLUMN vehicle_type_id INTEGER;
ALTER TABLE claims ADD COLUMN outstation_city_id INTEGER;
ALTER TABLE claims ADD COLUMN from_city_id INTEGER;
ALTER TABLE claims ADD COLUMN to_city_id INTEGER;
ALTER TABLE claims ADD COLUMN status_id INTEGER;
DO NOT add FK constraints yet - data migration first

migrations.purpose: Populate new ID columns from existing text values
content: UPDATE claims c SET designation_id = d.designation_id FROM designations d WHERE c.designation = d.designation_name;
UPDATE claims c SET work_location_id = wl.location_id FROM work_locations wl WHERE c.work_location::text = wl.location_code;
UPDATE claims c SET vehicle_type_id = vt.vehicle_type_id FROM vehicle_types vt WHERE c.vehicle_type::text = vt.vehicle_code;
UPDATE claims c SET status_id = cs.status_id FROM claim_statuses cs WHERE c.status = cs.status_code;
-- Migrate city names to city_ids (requires city name matching)
UPDATE claims c SET outstation_city_id = ct.city_id FROM cities ct WHERE c.outstation_location = ct.city_name;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM claims WHERE status_id IS NULL; -- Should be 0

migrations.purpose: Add FK constraints after data migration
content: ALTER TABLE claims ADD CONSTRAINT fk_claims_designation FOREIGN KEY (designation_id) REFERENCES designations(designation_id);
ALTER TABLE claims ADD CONSTRAINT fk_claims_work_location FOREIGN KEY (work_location_id) REFERENCES work_locations(location_id);
ALTER TABLE claims ADD CONSTRAINT fk_claims_vehicle_type FOREIGN KEY (vehicle_type_id) REFERENCES vehicle_types(vehicle_type_id);
ALTER TABLE claims ADD CONSTRAINT fk_claims_status FOREIGN KEY (status_id) REFERENCES claim_statuses(status_id);
ALTER TABLE claims ADD CONSTRAINT fk_claims_outstation_city FOREIGN KEY (outstation_city_id) REFERENCES cities(city_id);
ALTER TABLE claims ADD CONSTRAINT fk_claims_from_city FOREIGN KEY (from_city_id) REFERENCES cities(city_id);
ALTER TABLE claims ADD CONSTRAINT fk_claims_to_city FOREIGN KEY (to_city_id) REFERENCES cities(city_id);
Create indexes on new FK columns

migrations.purpose: Remove old text-based columns after verification
migrations.critical_warning: ONLY execute after extensive testing of ID-based system
content: -- Verify ID-based system working for 2+ weeks
ALTER TABLE claims DROP COLUMN designation;
ALTER TABLE claims DROP COLUMN work_location;
ALTER TABLE claims DROP COLUMN vehicle_type;
ALTER TABLE claims DROP COLUMN status;
ALTER TABLE claims DROP COLUMN outstation_location; -- text city name
-- Keep text columns in backup table for safety

migrations.purpose: Create detailed expense line items table
content: One row per taxi bill, fuel entry, accommodation, etc.

correct_approach: if (designation_id === 4) - CORRECT but still not ideal
BEST: Query approval_routing table with employee_id

workflow_apis: GET /api/workflow/required-approvers?claim_id={id} - Get approval routing for claim
POST /api/workflow/transition - Execute status transition with validation
claim_apis: POST /api/claims/calculate - Calculate claim total using DB rates
POST /api/claims/validate - Validate claim against DB rules
GET /api/claims/my-approvals - Claims waiting for my approval (using employee_id)
GET /api/claims?status_id={id} - Filter by status_id not status text

responsibilities: getEmployeeById(employee_id) - Never use email as lookup
getEmployeeRoles(employee_id) - Get all roles for employee
getEmployeeApprovers(employee_id) - Get approval chain using routing table
getRequiredApprovers(claim_id) - Query approval_routing by designation_id + state_id
canUserApprove(user_employee_id, claim_id) - Check if user has required role
executeTransition(claim_id, to_status_id, user_employee_id, comments) - With validation
calculateClaimTotal(claim_data) - Query expense_rates table, NO magic numbers

caching_strategy: Use React Server Components cache for config data
Cache duration: 1 hour for master tables
Cache invalidation: On config updates via admin panel

zod_schema_refactoring.current_problem: z.enum(['State Business Head', ...]) with hardcoded values
zod_schema_refactoring.solution: Dynamic schema factories that fetch from DB

usage: -- Cities dropdown filtered by selected state_id

status_display.current_problem: Hardcoded status badges with text matching

logic: Fetch claim_statuses once on page load
Display status_name with display_color from DB
NO hardcoded status text or colors

form_validation.current_problem: Frontend duplicates backend validation with hardcoded rules

solution.approach: Frontend provides UX hints, backend enforces rules
implementation: Fetch validation_rules from API on form load
Show hints like 'Maximum 7 days' using fetched rules
On submit, backend validates using DB rules
Display backend validation errors to user
NO hardcoded validation logic in frontend

solution.approach: Backend provides user permissions via API
implementation: Frontend checks permissions array, not role names
Buttons enabled/disabled based on allowed_transitions API

claim_form_component: Replace all <option> hardcoded values with DynamicSelect
Remove all hardcoded validation - call /api/claims/validate on change
Remove calculation logic - call /api/claims/calculate for preview
approval_dashboard: Fetch claims using GET /api/claims/my-approvals (uses employee_id not email)
Display status using StatusBadge component (status_id based)
NO hardcoded status checks or permission logic
admin_panel: CRUD for all master tables (designations, states, cities, etc.)
ALL changes via API - cache invalidation automatic

unit_tests: Employee service functions with employee_id lookups
Approval service routing logic using IDs
integration_tests: Full claim submission flow with ID-based workflow
Multi-level approval flow (L1 → L2 → L3 → L4)
Employee role changes don't break approvals
Designation changes snapshot correctly in claims
Vehicle permission checks work for all designations
migration_tests: All existing claims migrated with correct IDs
No orphaned records after enum migration
regression_tests: All existing functionality works with ID-based system
Calculations produce same results as before

tasks: Create all master tables and seed data
Create API endpoints for config data
TESTING: Verify all tables populated correctly
phase_1_infrastructure.deliverable: Complete ID-based database schema with API layer

tasks: Implement all service layers (employee, approval, calculation, validation, config)
Replace all text-based lookups with ID lookups
Replace all hardcoded constants with DB queries
TESTING: 100% unit test coverage for services
phase_2_backend_refactoring.deliverable: Backend fully ID-based with zero hardcoding

tasks: TESTING: All existing claims work with new schema
phase_3_claims_migration.deliverable: Claims table fully migrated to ID-based FKs
phase_3_claims_migration.rollback_plan: Documented procedure to restore from backup

tasks: Refactor all claim forms to use API-fetched data
TESTING: E2E tests for all user flows
phase_4_frontend_refactoring.deliverable: Frontend fully dumb, all data from APIs

tasks: Build admin UI for master table management
TESTING: Admin can modify all config without code changes
Execute migration 124 (drop old text columns) after 2+ weeks of stability

database_level: ✅ All 20+ master tables created with full seed data
✅ All FKs use integer IDs - ZERO email or text FKs
✅ Claims table uses status_id, designation_id, location_id, vehicle_type_id, city_id FKs
✅ Approval routing table replaces email-based routing
backend_level: ✅ ZERO grep matches for: designation === 'State Business Head'
✅ ZERO grep matches for: user.email === approver_email
✅ ZERO grep matches for: status === 'Pending Approval'
✅ ZERO const arrays with business data
✅ All validation reads from validation_rules table
✅ All calculations read from expense_rates table
✅ All permissions check via role_id not email
✅ Dynamic Zod schemas query DB for enums
frontend_level: ✅ All forms validate via backend APIs
workflow_level: ✅ Approvers determined by approval_routing table using employee_id
✅ Status transitions validated against claim_status_transitions table
✅ Approval history uses approver_employee_id not email
✅ Can query 'claims pending at L2' using status_id and approval_level
✅ Can query 'claims I approved' using employee_id
scalability_level: ✅ Employee promotion changes designation_id - all claims still work
✅ Employee leaving company marked inactive - historical data intact
✅ Renaming designation updates one row - zero code changes
✅ Adding new status adds rows to claim_statuses - zero code changes
✅ Modifying approval flow updates routing table - zero code changes
✅ Changing expense rates updates expense_rates - zero code changes
✅ Admin panel allows all config changes without developer

migration_files: Each with complete SQL, seed data, indexes, RLS policies
documentation: docs/admin/configuration-guide.md - How to use admin panel
docs/rollback/rollback-procedure.md - How to rollback if needed

mandatory_mcp_calls: Use Filesystem MCP to inspect existing schema before migrations
Use Supabase MCP to verify table structure after each migration
Use Supabase MCP to validate data after migration 122 (claims ID migration)
Use Next.js MCP to locate all files using text-based lookups
Use Git MCP to commit each migration separately
Use Playwright MCP to test UI after frontend refactoring
Use memory MCP to track migration progress and issues
mcp_workflow: BEFORE each migration: Supabase MCP inspect current schema
AFTER each migration: Supabase MCP verify new schema
AFTER each migration: Supabase MCP query new tables to verify data
BEFORE backend refactor: Filesystem MCP locate files to change
AFTER backend refactor: grep for text-based patterns to verify removed
BEFORE frontend refactor: Next.js MCP understand component structure
AFTER frontend refactor: Playwright MCP test UI flows

final_instructions.tone: This is a COMPLETE architectural transformation. Every single text-based reference must be eliminated. Every single hardcoded value must move to database. Zero tolerance for shortcuts.
final_instructions.approach: Execute migrations in strict order. Test extensively at each phase. Document everything. Use MCP religiously.
final_instructions.quality: Follow copilot-instructions.md: file size limits, TypeScript strict mode, proper error handling, RLS policies, indexes, tests.
final_instructions.mindset: Database is source of truth. IDs are keys, not text. Employees change jobs, emails change, roles change - system must handle gracefully. Surface-level config changes should NEVER require code changes.
final_instructions.output: Provide COMPLETE migration SQL files, COMPLETE service implementations, COMPLETE API routes, COMPLETE component refactors. No TODOs. No placeholders. Production-ready code.
final_instructions.testing: Every migration tested. Every service unit tested. Every API integration tested. Every UI flow E2E tested. Rollback procedure documented and tested.
final_instructions.no_assumptions: Use MCP to verify current state before every change. Never assume schema. Never assume data. Never assume code structure.
